---
title: "Execution Modes"
subtitle: "`execution_mode`, `strict_dependency`, and `budget` - choose failure behavior deliberately."
sidebar: concepts
---

## What it is

Execution settings separate two decisions: whether a feature is allowed into
the plan, and what happens if its named backend is missing at runtime. Optional
backends are excluded from the base installation when they require large model
weights, API credentials, extra Python packages, or system programs such as
ffmpeg and Tesseract.

## Planning budgets

| Budget | Planning effect |
|---|---|
| `default` | base routes that do not require opt-in |
| `allow_python` | optional Python-backed routes, excluding placeholders |
| `all` | all registered routes, including costly routes that still may need dependencies |

## Runtime modes

| Setting | Runtime effect |
|---|---|
| omitted (default) | use the named method; raise if its backend is unavailable |
| `execution_mode="strict"` | explicitly select the default fail-fast behavior |
| `execution_mode="fallback"` | explicitly request a deterministic proxy with different semantics and provenance, where one exists |
| `strict_dependency=True` | compatibility flag for strict dependency behavior |
| `strict_dependency=False` | compatibility alias for explicitly requested fallback behavior |

## Invariants

- Budget checks happen during planning.
- Named methods fail rather than silently returning a different computation.
- Runtime fallback/strict behavior is represented in extractor parameters.
- Explicit fallback outputs annotate the substituted backend and reason in
  provenance metadata where implemented.

## What it refuses

- Unsupported budget names.
- Strict optional backends without their required packages, models, or system tools.

## What it doesn't do

These settings do not download models automatically. For model-backed
extractors, local cache policy is controlled by extractor parameters such as
`local_files_only`. Fallback mode is a development convenience, not evidence
that the named scientific method ran.

## See also

[Media and optional backends](../start/05-media-and-optional-backends.qmd) · [Feature catalogue](feature-catalogue.qmd)
